items.title: CVE-2026-35273: Oracle PeopleSoft RCE Zero-Day (CVSS 9.8) Exploited by ShinyHunters — 100+ Orgs Breached
items.summary: An unauthenticated RCE bug in Oracle PeopleSoft PeopleTools 8.61/8.62 (CVSS 9.8) was weaponized by ShinyHunters (tracked by Mandiant as UNC6240) as a zero-day from May 27 to June 9, hitting 100+ orgs — 68% in higher education. Oracle issued an out-of-band advisory June 10. Attackers deployed MeshCentral for persistence, used zstd compression, and exfiltrated data over SSH to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
items.why: A 9.8 CVSS zero-day exploited for two weeks with no patch — PeopleSoft stores sensitive student and HR data. Emergency patching is critical; delay equals confirmed breach exposure.

items.title: GreatXML: Unpatched BitLocker Bypass via Windows Defender Offline Scan Yields SYSTEM Shell in Recovery Mode
items.summary: Researcher Chaotic Eclipse published GreatXML on June 11 — an unpatched exploit that bypasses BitLocker by placing two XML files (unattend.xml + ReAgent.xml) on the recovery partition, then booting into WinRE. Any Windows machine that has run a Defender Offline Scan is vulnerable to brief physical-access exploitation, granting an unrestricted SYSTEM shell with full access to the decrypted BitLocker volume. No patch exists as of June 12.
items.why: BitLocker is a last line of defense for stolen laptops. This technique abuses a trusted Windows workflow, requires no credentials, and yields full disk access — immediate risk assessment needed for endpoint security policies.

items.title: CVE-2026-50751: Check Point VPN Critical Auth Bypass (CVSS 9.3) Actively Exploited by Qilin Ransomware Gang
items.summary: A logic flaw in IKEv1 certificate validation in Check Point Remote Access VPN (CVSS 9.3) lets unauthenticated attackers establish full VPN sessions without credentials. Active exploitation began May 7 and spiked in early June. Qilin ransomware affiliates have confirmed post-compromise intrusions via this vector across dozens of global organizations. Check Point released an emergency hotfix; mitigation includes disabling IKEv1 and enforcing IKEv2-only.
items.why: VPN authentication bypass at 9.3 CVSS is a ransomware entry point at scale. Organizations using Check Point products with IKEv1 enabled must apply the hotfix on an emergency basis before attackers pivot to ransomware deployment.

items.title: CVE-2026-11645: Chrome V8 Out-of-Bounds Zero-Day (CVSS 8.8) Exploited in Wild — CISA Mandates Patch by June 23
items.summary: An out-of-bounds memory access in Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine (CVSS 8.8) is confirmed actively exploited. Google patched it June 8 in Chrome 149.0.7827.102 across all platforms. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog requiring federal agency remediation by June 23. The discovering researcher received a $55,000 bug bounty. Exploitation enables arbitrary code execution inside the browser renderer sandbox via a crafted HTML page.
items.why: Actively exploited browser zero-days allow drive-by RCE with no user interaction beyond page visit. Enterprise Chrome fleets need emergency update enforcement — the CISA KEV deadline signals high-confidence exploitation activity.

items.title: Iranian APT Screening Serpens Deploys MiniUpdate & MiniJunk V2 RATs via AppDomainManager Hijacking Against US, Israel, UAE
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit42 tracked Iran-linked Screening Serpens (aka Smoke Sandstorm / UNC1549) deploying two new RAT families — MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 — in active espionage operations targeting entities in the US, Israel, UAE, and other Middle Eastern nations. Infection chains begin with spear-phishing, use DLL sideloading, and leverage AppDomainManager hijacking for stealthy execution. C2 traffic is routed through 3–5 Azure-hosted domains per target. April variants expanded the command dispatcher to 18 opcodes.
items.why: AppDomainManager hijacking is a defense evasion technique that bypasses many EDR products. Azure-hosted C2 blends with legitimate traffic. The campaign's acceleration since February 2026 signals increased Iranian cyber operations tied to regional conflict.
